title: Cephalaria appendiculata
taxonomy: Cephalaria appendiculata is classified within the family Caprifoliaceae and the order Dipsacales. It is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The plant belongs to the genus Cephalaria, which includes species of flowering plants. Cephalaria appendiculata is often grouped under the subgenus Cephalaria due to its unique characteristics. This classification is based on the plant's morphology and phylogenetic analysis.
uses: Cephalaria appendiculata has been traditionally used in medicine due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant has been found to have various bioactive compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may contribute to its medicinal effects.
